2026-03.title: India Begins 100-Day Countdown to International Yoga Day 2026
2026-03.summary: The Ministry of Ayush and Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga are organizing Yoga Mahotsav-2026 on March 13, 2026, marking the 100-day countdown to International Day of Yoga (IDY) 2026. This initiative involves 100 organizations promoting yoga in 100 cities. The program aims to expand outreach, encourage public participation, and prepare for the 12th IDY, scheduled for commemoration at 100 iconic locations, inspiring holistic well-being through yoga. Preparations are gaining momentum.

2026-03.title: India Proposes Drugs Rules Amendment to Streamline Blood Product Viral Testing
2026-03.summary: India's MoHFW seeks public comments on amending the Drugs Rules, 1945, to streamline blood product viral testing. The proposed change aims to remove duplicate testing of finished products, as pooled plasma is already rigorously screened for HIV, Hepatitis B, and C as per global pharmacopoeial standards. This aligns regulations, rationalizes testing, reduces compliance burden, and upholds patient safety.

2026-03.title: TRAI Assesses Network Quality at Key Puducherry Medical Facilities
2026-03.summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) assessed mobile network quality at key healthcare facilities in Puducherry, including JIPMER Medical College and Rajiv Gandhi Government Women and Children Hospital, during its February 2026 independent drive test. Findings covered voice and data services from Airtel, BSNL, RJIL, and VIL, crucial for operational efficiency and emergency communications. This assessment highlights the importance of reliable telecom infrastructure for supporting public health services in the region.

2026-04.title: NSO 80th Round Survey Shows Transformative Progress in India's Healthcare
2026-04.summary: The NSO 80th Round survey reveals significant improvements in India's healthcare access, marked by a threefold increase in government insurance coverage and rising institutional deliveries. Public health facility utilization has grown, particularly in rural areas, while median outpatient expenditure remains zero. These findings underscore the government's commitment to affordable healthcare, effectively reducing the financial burden of out-of-pocket expenses for citizens.

2026-04.title: India Shifts Focus to Combat Non-Communicable Diseases
2026-04.summary: Union Health Minister JP Nadda highlighted India's transition toward a holistic health policy to combat rising non-communicable diseases. The government is accelerating NCD control through expanded infrastructure and massive screening drives, having already screened millions for hypertension, diabetes, and cancer. The strategy emphasizes that 70% of NCD risk factors are lifestyle-driven, urging a shift toward prevention and healthier living.

2026-04.title: NHA Concludes Chintan Shivir on Digital Health
2026-04.summary: The National Health Authority concluded a two-day Chintan Shivir in Pune focusing on AB PM-JAY and ABDM. The event highlighted innovation and cooperative governance, showcasing Karnataka's online referral system and Gujarat's clinical governance mechanisms. The initiative aims to strengthen healthcare delivery through data-driven decision-making and digital verification, ensuring improved clinical appropriateness and cost efficiencies across various Indian States and UTs.

2026-04.title: NHA Holds Chintan Shivir to Accelerate Digital Health
2026-04.summary: The National Health Authority convened a two-day Chintan Shivir in Pune to review and accelerate the implementation of AB PM-JAY and ABDM. The event focused on preventive, technology-driven healthcare and unveiled new guidelines and the NHCX strategy. Officials aim to strengthen digital health architecture and build a future-ready workforce to streamline health claims and improve overall healthcare delivery systems.

2026-04.title: MoHFW Launches Clinical Workflow App for CHOs
2026-04.summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched an ICMR-developed mobile application to streamline clinical workflows for Community Health Officers at Sub-Centre Ayushman Arogya Mandirs. Aligned with 12 CPHC service packages, the app provides structured decision-support tools and checklists for patient assessment. This digital intervention aims to enhance primary healthcare delivery, ensuring timely referrals and informed clinical decision-making nationwide.

2026-04.title: NHA Launches Auto-Adjudication Hackathon for AB PM-JAY
2026-04.summary: The National Health Authority has invited innovators to the AB PM-JAY Auto-Adjudication Hackathon 2026 to develop digital solutions for claims processing. With over 2,600 participants registered, the initiative seeks to leverage AI and automation to reduce manual workloads and fraud. The goal is to create a scalable, transparent, and future-ready framework to handle the high volume of daily health claims.

2026-04.title: Union Health Minister Marks World Health Day
2026-04.summary: Union Minister Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda extended greetings on World Health Day, emphasizing the government's commitment to preventive and promotive healthcare. He highlighted the synergy between traditional wisdom and modern science, citing Ayushman Bharat and PM Bharatiya Janaushadhi Pariyojana as transformative initiatives. The Minister praised frontline healthcare workers for their dedication in making quality treatment accessible and affordable for citizens.

2026-05.title: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Surpasses 90 Crore ABHA Accounts
2026-05.summary: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission has achieved a major milestone, crossing 90 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHAs) in 2026. Growth surged from 14.7 crore in 2021 to 90 crore, with Uttar Pradesh leading at 15.3 crore. These 14-digit digital identities enable secure, consent-based health record sharing, with women accounting for nearly half of all registered ABHA holders.

2026-05.title: India Releases National Family Health Survey-6
2026-05.summary: The NFHS-6 (2023-24) reveals significant progress in maternal and child health. Institutional deliveries reached 90.6%, and antenatal care coverage rose to 95.9%. Full immunization coverage improved from 83.8% to 87.1%, while child nutrition indicators showed positive trends, with stunting reduced by 17% and severe wasting by 32%. The survey covered 6.79 lakh households across 715 Indian districts.

2026-05.title: India Launches Tobacco-Free Educational Institutions (ToFEI) Application
2026-05.summary: To mark World No Tobacco Day 2026, Union Health Secretary Punya Salila Srivastava launched the Tobacco-Free Educational Institutions (ToFEI) pilot application. The digital tool monitors tobacco-free norms and prohibits sales within 100 yards of campuses. Initially piloting in Rajasthan, Meghalaya, and Maharashtra, the app facilitates self-assessment and reporting to protect students from nicotine addiction and ensure healthier learning environments.

2026-05.title: National Health Accounts Estimates 2022-23 Released
2026-05.summary: India's Government Health Expenditure (GHE) grew threefold from ₹1.30 lakh crores in 2013-14 to ₹3.85 lakh crores in 2022-23. GHE as a share of GDP rose from 1.15% to 1.43%. Notably, out-of-pocket expenditure declined from 64.2% to 43.4% of total health spending, while government spending on primary healthcare more than doubled to ₹1.4 lakh crores over the decade.

2026-05.title: India Intensifies Ebola Preparedness Measures
2026-05.summary: Union Health Minister Shri J P Nadda reviewed Ebola preparedness following WHO's declaration of a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. While India reports zero cases of Bundibugyo Ebola, the government has intensified screening at international airports. The ICMR and NCDC are on high alert for tracking and testing, with updated SOPs shared across all States and Union Territories.

2026-05.title: India Advises Travel Caution Amid Ebola Outbreak
2026-05.summary: The WHO declared the Bundibugyo strain Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern on 17 May 2026. With high mortality and no approved vaccines, India advises citizens to avoid non-essential travel to DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan. Authorities are strengthening surveillance at entry points to detect febrile illnesses from these high-risk affected areas.

2026-05.title: ABDM Achieves 100 Crore Linked Health Records
2026-05.summary: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission has reached a milestone of 100 crore health records linked to ABHA accounts. This represents a doubling of records from 50 crore in February 2025 within just 15 months. Over 450 public and private health tech solutions are now integrated, facilitating a secure, paperless, and interoperable digital healthcare ecosystem across various Indian states.

2026-05.title: India Showcases TB Screening Efforts at WHA
2026-05.summary: At the 79th World Health Assembly, Union Health Minister J P Nadda highlighted India's 'TB-Mukt Bharat' vision. The initiative utilizes AI-assisted tools, digital chest X-rays, and the 'Khushi' multilingual chatbot to enhance early detection. India is deploying decentralized testing systems to reduce diagnostic delays in underserved regions, aiming to build a resilient, people-centered health system for lung screening.

2026-05.title: ICMR and ICAR Launch SEHAT Mission
2026-05.summary: The Indian Council of Medical Research and Indian Council of Agricultural Research launched 'SEHAT', a national mission-mode programme. This initiative focuses on agriculture-nutrition-health convergence to shift India from curative to preventive healthcare. The programme prioritises biofortified crops, integrated farming systems, and NCD prevention, aiming to translate agricultural advancements into measurable public health outcomes through a whole-of-government and whole-of-system approach.

2026-05.title: NHA Concludes AB PM-JAY AI Hackathon
2026-05.summary: The National Health Authority, IndiaAI Mission, and IISc Bengaluru concluded the AB PM-JAY Auto-Adjudication Hackathon Showcase 2026. Over 3,500 participants competed to develop AI-driven solutions for health claims adjudication to enhance transparency and speed. Winning teams received cash awards up to ₹5 lakh, with the NHA focusing on scaling these innovations to prevent fraud and improve programme integrity.

2026-06.title: Launch of SUMAN Roadmap 2030 for Maternal and Newborn Health
2026-06.summary: Union Health Minister Jagat Prakash Nadda launched the SUMAN Roadmap 2030, a strategic framework designed to transform maternal and newborn healthcare in India. Aimed at achieving Sustainable Development Goals by 2030, the roadmap addresses persistent mortality challenges in high-focus states through an evidence-driven, targeted approach. Anchored in the RMNCHA+N framework, it employs a comprehensive life-cycle strategy, integrating critical health interventions from pre-pregnancy through childbirth and the postnatal period. This initiative seeks to ensure equitable, high-quality care by combining national priorities with local realities to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality across all diverse geographies.

2026-06.title: Introduction of Samagra Shishu Bal Swasthya Karyakram (SSBSK)
2026-06.summary: The Samagra Shishu Bal Swasthya Karyakram (SSBSK) was launched to provide a seamless continuum of home and community-based care for children from birth to 36 months. By integrating Home-Based Newborn Care (HBNC) and Home-Based Care for Young Child (HBYC), the program emphasizes brain development and survival. A key community medicine feature is the risk-stratified approach for 'At-risk' children—such as those with low birth weight or malnutrition—who receive intensified follow-up. This includes up to nine home visits in the first 42 days for newborns and eight visits up to 36 months for children.

2026-06.title: Evolution of Anemia Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan Guidelines
2026-06.summary: The Anemia Mukt Bharat program has transitioned into the Anemia Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan, shifting focus from mere prophylactic supplementation to comprehensive therapeutic care. The new operational guidelines introduce the 'T4 Strategy'—Test, Treat, Talk, and Track—to strengthen screening and follow-up. The expanded 7x7x7 framework now includes low birth weight babies as a new beneficiary group. Integrating 'Jan Chetna' for community involvement and 'Eating Right' initiatives, the program also launches a unified digital portal for tracking and analysis, ensuring a robust institutional mechanism to combat anemia through a continuum of care across India.

2026-06.title: India Expands QR Code Drug Traceability Framework
2026-06.summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare amended the Drugs Rules, 1945, expanding Schedule H2 to include vaccines, antimicrobials, anti-cancer drugs, and NDPS substances. Manufacturers must now affix QR codes to packaging. These codes store nine key data elements, including batch numbers and expiry dates, to enhance the safety, quality, and authentication of the pharmaceutical supply chain across India.

2026-06.title: Launch of AIR SUVIDHA 2.0 for Ebola Screening
2026-06.summary: The Ministry of Civil Aviation and DIAL launched AIR SUVIDHA 2.0 to monitor Ebola health risks at Indian entry points. Following the WHO's May 17, 2026, emergency declaration, the portal requires international travelers to submit a health self-declaration 24 hours before arrival. It tracks 21-day travel history and symptoms, enabling real-time coordination between health and immigration agencies.

2026-06.title: Union Health Minister Reviews TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan Progress
2026-06.summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda reviewed the TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan, noting that 28 crore people were screened and 20 lakh received preventive treatment since December 2024. X-ray screening increased 34-fold via handheld machines. With 1.7 lakh shivirs covering 1.2 lakh high-risk areas and 1.9 lakh volunteers, India's TB incidence declined to 187 cases per lakh population in 2024.

2026-06.title: India Strengthens Dengue and Malaria Preparedness for Monsoon
2026-06.summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda chaired a high-level meeting to assess national preparedness against dengue and malaria ahead of the monsoon season. He urged States and UTs to strengthen surveillance systems for early detection and ensure the adequate availability of medicines, diagnostic facilities, and hospital beds. The focus remains on strict adherence to prevention, diagnosis, and treatment protocols nationwide.

2026-06.title: India Strengthens Strategies for Leprosy-Free Vision
2026-06.summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare convened a regional workshop in Chhattisgarh to achieve zero leprosy transmission. While India attained national elimination as a public health problem in 2005, transmission persists in endemic hotspots. Officials emphasized early diagnosis, periodic case detection campaigns, and expanding Single-Dose Rifampicin coverage for eligible healthy contacts to interrupt the disease's transmission cycle effectively.

2026-06.title: PMSMA Marks Decade of Care with Major Health Gains
2026-06.summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda launched celebrations for 10 years of Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyaan. India achieved a significant 86% reduction in maternal mortality, outpacing the global average. Key health indicators show institutional deliveries have risen to 90.6%, while antenatal care coverage has reached 95.9%, reflecting the government's commitment to ensuring safe motherhood and healthy newborns.

2026-06.title: West Bengal Joins Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Scheme
2026-06.summary: West Bengal has become the 36th State or Union Territory to implement the Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY scheme. Following a Memorandum of Understanding signed between the National Health Authority and the state government, health assurance benefits will now extend to approximately 1.43 crore families. This landmark inclusion marks a major step toward achieving universal health coverage and financial protection for citizens.

2026-06.title: India Celebrates Decade of Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan
2026-06.summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched nationwide celebrations on June 9, 2026, marking ten years of the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan. To commemorate a decade of safe motherhood, Union Minister J.P. Nadda released a ₹75 commemorative coin and a ₹5 postal stamp. The initiative continues promoting nine assured free antenatal care services to ensure healthy pregnancies.

2026-06.title: West Bengal Joins Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Scheme
2026-06.summary: West Bengal is set to become the 36th State or Union Territory to implement the Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana. A Memorandum of Understanding will be signed between the National Health Authority and the West Bengal government. This expansion of the world's largest publicly funded health assurance scheme aims to provide equitable and accessible healthcare for all citizens.
